policy.title: RBI Issues Final Guidelines on Climate Risk Management for Scheduled Commercial Banks
policy.description: RBI released binding guidelines requiring banks with assets over ₹10,000 crore to integrate climate scenario analysis into ICAAP by March 2027. Banks must report physical risk exposures across top 10 sectors with granular district-level hazard mapping.

policy.title: SEBI Extends BRSR Core to Top 500 Listed Companies, Adds Climate Transition Plans
policy.description: SEBI mandated BRSR Core for top 500 companies by market cap starting FY27, adding mandatory climate transition plan disclosures with Scope 3 emissions for high-impact sectors. Third-party assurance now required for all quantitative ESG metrics.

policy.title: IRDAI Releases Sandbox Framework for Index-Based Climate Insurance Products
policy.description: IRDAI opened a regulatory sandbox for parametric insurance covering flood, cyclone, and heat stress perils, allowing 18-month testing with relaxed solvency requirements. Insurers must use ISRO or IMD-certified trigger data sources.

policy.title: Basel Committee Finalizes Pillar 3 Climate Risk Disclosure Standards
policy.description: BCBS published final Pillar 3 disclosure requirements for climate-related financial risks effective January 2028, mandating standardized templates for physical and transition risk exposures. Large internationally active banks must quantify credit risk by climate scenario.

policy.title: NGFS Releases Phase V Climate Scenarios with Enhanced Physical Risk Pathways
policy.description: NGFS published updated scenarios incorporating latest IPCC AR7 data with higher-resolution regional physical risk variables for South Asia. New scenarios include a 'fragmented policy' pathway modeling divergent carbon pricing regimes across jurisdictions.
policy.source: Network for Greening the Financial System

policy.title: EFRAG Publishes Sector-Specific ESRS for Financial Institutions
policy.description: EFRAG finalized sector standards for banks and insurers under CSRD, requiring financed emissions disclosure using PCAF methodology and portfolio alignment metrics by 2027 reporting. Standards apply to Indian companies with significant EU revenues.

competitors.title: Jupiter Intelligence Partners with HDFC Bank for Physical Risk Assessment Platform
competitors.description: Jupiter announced a multi-year partnership with HDFC Bank to deploy its ClimateScore platform for portfolio-level physical risk assessment across commercial lending. The deal reportedly covers 15 states and integrates with HDFC's loan origination system.

competitors.title: Sprih Raises $18M Series B to Expand Climate Risk Analytics for Indian Banks
competitors.description: Bangalore-based Sprih closed Series B led by Peak XV Partners, adding physical risk modeling to its ESG platform. The company plans to onboard 25 Indian banks and NBFCs by December 2026 with RBI-aligned climate disclosure tools.

competitors.title: Moody's Launches India-Specific Physical Risk Scores Covering 750 Districts
competitors.description: Moody's released district-level physical risk scores for India covering flood, cyclone, drought, and heat stress with integration into its credit risk models. The product targets Indian banks preparing for RBI climate risk compliance.

competitors.title: XDI Expands APAC Coverage with Asset-Level Physical Risk Data for South Asia
competitors.description: XDI launched granular physical risk analytics covering 12 million commercial assets across India, Bangladesh, and Sri Lanka. The platform now includes forward-looking damage functions calibrated to South Asian construction standards.

competitors.title: Watershed Partners with Tata Group for Enterprise Carbon Accounting Rollout
competitors.description: Watershed announced partnership with Tata Sons to deploy carbon accounting across 15 Tata Group companies, marking its largest India engagement. The deal includes Scope 3 supplier data collection across Tata's manufacturing supply chains.

competitors.title: GIST Advisory Launches Natural Capital Risk Module for Indian Lenders
competitors.description: GIST Advisory released a nature-related financial risk module aligned with TNFD framework, targeting Indian banks with agricultural and MSME lending portfolios. The tool covers water stress, biodiversity dependency, and land degradation metrics.

research.title: Physical Climate Risk in Indian Banking: Sectoral Exposure and Capital Implications
research.description: RBI's first systemic study estimates 8.2% of Indian bank assets face high physical climate risk exposure, with agriculture and MSMEs most vulnerable. The report models potential NPA increases of 120-180 bps under severe flood scenarios in eastern states.

research.title: Parametric Insurance for Climate Resilience in Emerging Markets
research.description: Swiss Re analysis shows parametric products could close 40% of South Asia's $12B annual climate protection gap, with flood and cyclone products showing highest viability. India's parametric market projected to reach $800M by 2030 with supportive regulation.

research.title: ESG Data Gaps in Emerging Markets: Implications for Financial Institutions
research.description: IFC study finds 67% of Indian SMEs lack basic ESG disclosure, creating a $2.3T green financing gap. Report recommends alternative data approaches including satellite imagery and supply chain tracing to enable ESG assessments for underbanked segments.

research.title: Climate Scenarios for Credit Risk: A Framework for Asian Banks
research.description: UNEPFI released practical guidance for Asian banks on translating NGFS scenarios into PD/LGD adjustments, with India case studies showing potential 25-40% LGD increases for coastal infrastructure under 2°C scenarios by 2050.

research.title: Basis Risk in Index-Based Agricultural Insurance: Evidence from India
research.description: World Bank evaluation of Indian crop insurance schemes finds basis risk reduces farmer payouts by 15-25% on average. Study recommends hybrid parametric-indemnity designs and higher-resolution trigger data to improve correlation with actual losses.

research.title: Flood Risk and Mortgage Portfolios: Lessons from South Asian Banks
research.description: FSB case studies from India and Bangladesh show 12-18% of mortgage collateral in major metros faces unpriced flood risk. Report calls for mandatory climate risk disclosure in property valuations and recommends stress testing at loan origination.

ourRead.headline: RBI's binding climate guidelines create a 12-month implementation cliff that will separate serious climate risk players from pretenders.
body: This month's signals converge on a single theme: the institutionalization of climate risk in Indian finance has moved from voluntary aspiration to regulatory mandate. RBI's March 28 guidelines requiring scenario analysis in ICAAP by March 2027, combined with Basel's Pillar 3 finalization and SEBI's BRSR Core expansion, create overlapping compliance demands that will force banks to invest seriously in physical risk analytics within the next 12 months.

The RBI guidelines are the most consequential policy development for StepChange in the past two years. The requirement for district-level hazard mapping across top 10 sectors is precisely what our PD/LGD modeling capability was designed for. However, the IRDAI sandbox framework also demands attention—mandating ISRO/IMD-certified trigger data sources signals regulatory preference for indigenous data providers, which positions us well against global competitors.

Jupiter's HDFC partnership is a direct competitive threat that validates our market timing but exposes our enterprise sales gap. Sprih's $18M raise with explicit RBI-aligned positioning shows domestic competitors are gearing up fast. However, Moody's India-specific risk scores and XDI's South Asia expansion reveal the real competitive pressure: global players are rapidly localizing. Our advantage lies in proprietary hazard data and parametric product design capability—areas where Jupiter and Moody's lack depth.

The RBI's own research showing 8.2% of bank assets at high physical risk exposure and potential 120-180 bps NPA increases is ammunition for every sales conversation. Pair this with UNEPFI's Asia-specific PD/LGD framework and we have a regulatory-backed methodology to anchor our models. The IFC's finding that 67% of Indian SMEs lack ESG disclosure reinforces our Global South data thesis—but we must accelerate alternative data integration before Moody's and MSCI fill the gap with lower-resolution proxies.

Watch for: which PSU banks move first on RBI compliance—they'll set procurement timelines for the sector. Action item: Schedule meetings with SBI, Bank of Baroda, and PNB risk teams before Q2 ends, positioning our district-level hazard data as the fastest path to ICAAP-ready scenario analysis.
